status_line: Tuesday 1:39 AM ET / Tue 05:39 UTC — quiet hour, all rhythm and no fresh artifacts. The mitigation flagged in last hour's push is in effect this run: this state is being composed inside a /tmp writable clone of origin/main rather than the local Desktop tree (which is 16 commits behind origin), so the local-vs-canonical drift that bit us at 04:40 cannot recur tonight. SARAH Run 137 (05:05 UTC) was a clean sweep — tenth consecutive zero-delta on the production tree, twelfth Downloads blackout, open-slate stable at 14 items (note: SARAH's tracker shows 14 across Runs 136–137; the 15-count from Run 134 has not surfaced in subsequent sweeps, so reconciling with prior state). Two infra cadence signals worth calling out: (1) jarvis-state.json heartbeat rebounded to 32,783 B at 04:48 UTC — first observed reading above the prior monotonic peak (26,815 B), recontextualizing Run 136's −14% shrink as a single-tick compaction anomaly rather than a downward trend; (2) JARVIS main repo .git/FETCH_HEAD advanced for the second consecutive hour with the offset to the heartbeat write tightening to 0.22s, consistent with a converging coordinated pull-then-write cycle. Creative wing has been silent for ~1h50m — no new emissions since the 03:48 UTC case-study-source-extracts packet. Ground truth on the creative side is unchanged from 04:45: GUARDIAN's 9.1/10 SHIPS CLEAN audit on MAESTRO's gallery-v2.pptx (02:51 UTC) still holds; WRITER's Lands' End cover letter v1 to Sarah Sylvester (03:08 UTC) is still send-ready; gobig.nyc 16-commit upgrade is shipped; three NOLAN storyboards (02:00–02:01) pair with the three shotlists. Lands' End T-2 days = Thu Apr 30 — letter + GUARDIAN-clean deck remain the morning-of priority. Next scheduled push: 06:39 UTC.

activity.text: Dashboard push — first run executed entirely inside a /tmp writable clone of origin/main per the mitigation flagged in 04:45's push, eliminating local-vs-canonical drift. Local Desktop tree confirmed 16 commits behind origin at start of run (was 12 last hour); fix-forward is no longer a concern this cycle because the JSON is composed against canonical HEAD, not against the stale local file. Quiet hour on the creative side — no new emissions since 03:48 UTC. Heartbeat rebounded above the prior monotonic peak at 04:48 UTC (32,783 B, ~22% above the 26,815 B peak). SARAH Run 137 clean at 05:05 UTC. Lands' End T-2 days, letter + GUARDIAN-clean deck send-ready.

Run 137 — clean quiet sweep, tenth consecutive zero-delta on the production tree. Two infra cadence signals: (1) jarvis-state.json heartbeat REBOUND ABOVE PRIOR PEAK — advanced to 32,783 B / 04:48 UTC (was 23,017 B / 03:43 UTC at Run 136), +9,766 B / +42%, pushing the file ~22% above the prior monotonic peak of 26,815 B (Runs 134/135). Recontextualizes Run 136's −14% shrink as a single-tick compaction anomaly rather than a downward trend. Cadence series: 21:38 → 22:42 → 23:43 → 00:46 → 01:47 → [missed ~02:48] → 03:43 → 04:48. (2) JARVIS main repo .git/FETCH_HEAD advanced 2nd consecutive hour to 04:48:49 UTC / 428 B (was 112 B at Run 136), with offset to heartbeat write tightening to 0.22s — coordinated pull-then-write cycle converging. Out of SARAH creative-asset domain on both. Open-slate stable at 14 items (no change from Run 136). Downloads inaccessible 12th consecutive run. Zero auto-moves/renames/deletes per conservative-attribution rail.

Fix-forward push — out-of-order 04:40:52 commit (8c56281) was built against a 12-commits-stale local clone and briefly clobbered the GUARDIAN-clean Lands' End picture (writeup said 'no new artifacts since 01:37 UTC' — wrong). Restored cda01e4 ground truth and bumped the timestamp. Root cause: the local Desktop repo's main branch is 12 commits behind origin because pushes from /tmp/jarvis-work clones don't pull-back into Desktop. Mitigation for next run: always git fetch + rebase the local Desktop tree (or build the JSON inside the writable clone) before composing the push. SARAH Run 136 quiet hour on the local mount.

Run 136 — quiet sweep on the local mounted scope, ninth consecutive zero-delta on the production tree. Two infra cadence signals against the LOCAL data/jarvis-state.json copy (not the GitHub canonical): heartbeat resumed after Run 135's missed tick at 03:43 UTC / 23,017 B (-3,798 B / -14% vs. local prior — first observed shrink); main JARVIS repo .git/FETCH_HEAD advanced to 03:44 UTC (first fetch in ~4h), one-second offset from the heartbeat consistent with a coordinated pipeline pull-then-write. Downloads inaccessible for the 11th consecutive run. Open-slate steady at 15 (carries Open Item #21 from Run 134). Note: the heartbeat shrink SARAH observed was on the LOCAL Desktop file — the GitHub canonical is on a different cadence/size. Worth reconciling.

case-study-source-extracts.md shipped (13,932 B at output/2026-04-27_gobig-upgrade/). Compiles the two case studies the gobig.nyc upgrade flagged as missing — Sharpie Gallery + Graco 'Next Arms' — into a single canonical packet: problem, insight, what-we-made, what-it-did, what-it-proves, tagline/hero line, visual cues (renders + storyboard frames + decks), credits, source docs. Doubles as a Cannes submission seed: Titanium / Brand Experience / PR / Film Craft / Direct / Outdoor positioning is captured for the Gallery; Graco Year-One credibility (3.5pt share, 1.5B impressions, 97% consider-to-purchase) is locked in for the Year-Two extension argument.

gobig.nyc status doc rev'd — Addendum 3 documents Session 4 (parallel worktree). Two commits on top of the 13: (1) 12a2eb0 — 'What It Proves' closing thesis band injected before .case-credits on 10 case studies (graco-safest-place, graco-next-arms, feel-the-joy, nuk-perfect-match, nuk-ms-rachel, world-is-your-canvas-tour, elmers-slime, creative-marker, diadem, ball-jar; roty + sharpie-gallery skipped intentionally — already close on multi-paragraph codas). (2) e962fdf — rich four-column footer rebuilt across all 21 pages with case-study auto-invert; /work closes on 'Bring us the brief everyone else is afraid of.' Total tonight: 16 commits, 21 pages, em-dash-clean, awards page truthful, hero re-tagged 'We imagine LOVEABLE brands. Then we make them real.'

Cross-reference nightly: 67 dashboard contacts × 11 enriched. 7 verified emails queued for Doc inline insert (Hinde, Cole, Hopkins, Jarjoura, Lacy, Hill, Brown). 1 job change confirmed (Ali Brown → Ventureland President, a.brown@venture.land). 4 Apollo no-email returns need alt paths (Dempster, Kuschnir, Thomas, Davies — LinkedIn InMail or warm intro). Doc lookup struck out — no master Drive Doc matches the 67-contact dashboard. Two Sheet candidates exist but neither mirrors the structure. Recommendation logged for Rick: confirm dashboard-as-source or create a Doc. Apollo MTD 39/60, 21 left for last 3 days of April.

Lands' End cover letter v1 shipped (output/2026-04-27_lands-end/cover-letter-sarah-sylvester-v1.md, 2,066 B). Addressed to Sarah Sylvester. Opens with the cultural moment — heritage-as-transgression in a disposability market. Cites Sharpie Gallery as proof of strategic range + Graco 'Next Arms' as proof of platform pivot in hardened categories. 30-minute ask, no deck unless wanted. Attachment: Sharpie Gallery Campaign Deck v2 (the GUARDIAN-validated 9.1 version). Send-ready for Tuesday/Wednesday morning.

Gallery Deck V2 full audit shipped (sharpie-guardian-gallery-deck-v2-audit-2026-04-27.md, 19,825 B). VERDICT: 9.1/10 SHIPS CLEAN. All five dimensions score 9 (Tone, Consistency, Clarity, Values Alignment, Emotional Impact). Voice locked: command-focused, cinematic, ownable. Katrina open earned (X-codes real, Sharpie survived, copy says so without hedging). No agency speak. Zero em dashes. One minor formatting note on Slide 24. First GUARDIAN-validated Lands' End pitch instrument — the urgency-lane v2 path closes clean; v1 deck + T2 v8 still uncovered.

Sharpie Gallery story bank v1 shipped (sharpie-scout-gallery-story-bank-v1.md, 69,443 B). 45 Cannes-ready narratives across 11 emotional categories. Sensitivity protocol for loss/queer/activism stories. First-9 hero shortlist (baseball glove, doorframe, barista cups, hospice signatures, cast signings, etc.). Casting strategy mixes ages 8–85, geography, profession. Pairs with the cultural insight memo (02:07 UTC).
